Quality Education.reason: Focus on 'independent research' and 'internationalization of Taiwan studies' aligns with Target 4.7.
As a doctoral course, it is the essence of quality tertiary education (Target 4.3), aiming to 'cultivate specialists' and develop 'independent research ability' (Target 4.4).
evidence: 本課程乃針對博士課程學生開設
培養學生獨立研究能力

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course aims to 'cultivate specialists in Taiwan studies', which tangentially relates to developing skills for employment (Target 8.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Directly addresses historical and systemic inequalities (Target 10.2, 10.3) through the intensive study of 'colonial theory' and postcolonialism.
evidence: ポストコロニアリズムの位相 (The Location of Postcolonialism)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Contributes to safeguarding cultural heritage (Target 11.4) by studying literary works such as '華麗島文学志' (A Literary History of the Beautiful Island).
evidence: 華麗島文学志－日本詩人の台湾体験

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: A minimal link exists through the instruction to 'Respect Copyright', which relates to responsible consumption of intellectual property, but not the core targets of this goal.
evidence: 維護智慧財產權，務必使用正版書籍

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The study of 'colonial theory' and postcolonialism directly analyzes historical injustices, contributing to the promotion of justice and non-discriminatory policies (Targets 16.3, 16.b).
evidence: ポストコロニアリズムの位相 (The Location of Postcolonialism)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Promotes international knowledge sharing (Target 17.6) through its goal of 'internationalization of Taiwan studies' and its 'comparative perspective' on different cultures.
